skill.name: testing-strategies
skill.description: Design comprehensive testing strategies for software quality assurance. Use when planning test coverage, implementing test pyramids, or setting up testing infrastructure. Handles unit testing, integration testing, E2E testing, TDD, and testing best practices.
skill.summary: Comprehensive testing strategy guide covering unit, integration, and E2E testing with TDD practices
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analyzer findings are false positives. The skill is pure documentation for testing strategies with no security concerns. Patterns detected (backtick execution, path traversal, weak crypto) are actually ASCII art diagrams, standard import paths, and educational content about string manipulation.

content.value_statement: Software projects lack consistent test coverage and face frequent bugs. This skill provides comprehensive testing strategies covering unit, integration, and E2E testing with practical examples and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Design test pyramid strategies with proper unit, integration, and E2E ratios
Implement unit tests using Given-When-Then pattern with Jest
Create integration tests for API endpoints using Supertest
Build E2E test scenarios with Playwright for user flows
Apply Test-Driven Development with Red-Green-Refactor cycles
Set up CI/CD testing pipelines with coverage requirements
limitations: Does not include specific testing framework installation instructions
Does not cover mobile application testing
Does not provide performance testing guidance

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Define testing strategy and infrastructure for a new software project from scratch

use_cases.title: Legacy Code Testing
use_cases.description: Add test coverage to existing codebase without proper tests
use_cases.target_user: Maintenance developers working on legacy systems

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: Set up automated testing in continuous integration workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Testing Strategy Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a testing strategy for my new {language} project. Include the test pyramid ratios, recommended tools, and how to structure unit, integration, and E2E tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project and need testing guidance

prompt_templates.title: Unit Testing Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write unit tests for a {function_name} function that {description}. Use Given-When-Then format and include edge cases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding unit tests to a specific function

prompt_templates.title: API Integration Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create integration tests for a {HTTP_method} endpoint at {endpoint_path}. Include tests for success case, validation errors, and duplicate handling.

prompt_templates.title: E2E User Flow Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Playwright E2E tests for the user registration flow including form validation, success redirect, and error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing complete user journeys in browser

output_examples.input: Create a testing strategy for a React dashboard application
output_examples.output:
The skill generates a comprehensive testing strategy document with:

- Test Pyramid: 70% Unit, 20% Integration, 10% E2E
- Tools: Jest (unit), Supertest (integration), Playwright (E2E)
- Coverage Goals: 80% unit, 60% integration
- CI/CD Integration steps for GitHub Actions
- Example test structures for components and API routes

output_examples.input: Write TDD tests for a password validator function
output_examples.output:
The skill provides:

- Red phase: Failing tests for password requirements
- Green phase: Minimal implementation
- Refactored solution with all validation rules
- Test cases covering length, special chars, numbers, uppercase

best_practices: Follow the AAA pattern: Arrange, Act, Assert in every test
Use descriptive test names that explain what should happen: 'should return error when email is invalid'
Test both happy path and sad path scenarios including edge cases
anti_patterns: Do not create tests that depend on execution order or other tests
Avoid using real databases in tests - use mocks or test databases
Do not use sleep or timeout-based assertions - use explicit waits instead

faq.question: What is the test pyramid?
faq.answer: The test pyramid is a concept that suggests having many fast unit tests at the base, fewer integration tests in the middle, and minimal E2E tests at the top. Recommended ratio is 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E.

faq.question: What tools does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers Jest for unit testing, Supertest for API integration testing, and Playwright for E2E browser testing.

faq.question: Can this skill help with TDD?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes detailed guidance on Test-Driven Development using the Red-Green-Refactor cycle.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for generating test implementations.

faq.question: How do I set up CI/CD testing?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance on integrating tests into GitHub Actions, including running unit tests on every commit and E2E tests before deployment.

faq.question: What coverage should I aim for?
faq.answer: Recommended targets are 80% code coverage for unit tests, 60% for integration tests, and critical user flows covered by E2E tests.
